title: Fail closed when adopter default branch is ambiguous
outOfScope: Inferring a default branch from local branch names without remote evidence.

sources.reason: Default branch discovery and fail-closed upgrade requirements.

riskAssessment.reason: An unknown remote default branch can cause upgrade or adoption to use the wrong base.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized the full implementation loop.

preReviewWarnings: Review missing remote HEAD, explicit base override, and local-only behavior.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Remote existence with unknown default branch fails closed unless explicit base remote and branch are supplied.
No-remote local-only repositories retain supported local behavior.
Tests and documentation cover remote, explicit, and missing evidence paths.
guidelines: Base identity is fail-closed when remote evidence is incomplete.
Explicit base overrides are recorded for review.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Known remote HEAD selects discovered base
Unknown remote HEAD requires explicit base evidence

intent.problem: Upgrade currently treats unknown remote default branch state like local-only operation.
constraints: Require explicit evidence when a remote exists but HEAD is ambiguous.
intent.rationale: Fail-closed base discovery prevents upgrades and adoption records from silently anchoring to an unverified branch.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the full implementation loop.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
